L1.area: Concurrent data structure and memory pool implementation layer

L2.description: A data race vulnerability exists due to an unconditional implementation of a thread-safety marker trait for a generic container type. Non-thread-safe types can be placed in this container and sent across thread boundaries, potentially leading to memory corruption.

L3.area: Thread safety trait implementations for the bucket-based pool storage types
L3.description: A data race vulnerability exists because a bucket-based pool storage container unconditionally implements the Send marker trait without requiring the contained type to also be Send. This allows non-thread-safe types like Cell or Rc to be sent across thread boundaries, potentially leading to memory corruption.

ground_truth.reason: The Bucket2<T> type unconditionally implements Send without requiring T: Send, allowing non-thread-safe types to be sent across thread boundaries, leading to potential data races and memory corruption.
